title: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (India)
description: Framework controls derived from the DPDP Act 2023. Each control maps to a specific section obligation for Data Fiduciaries and Significant Data Fiduciaries.

categories.notice: Sections 5-6 — Notice obligations to data principals
categories.consent: Section 6-7 — Consent collection, management, and withdrawal
categories.purpose: Section 4 — Purpose limitation and lawful processing bases
categories.security: Section 8 — Security safeguards and processor obligations
categories.breach: Section 8(6) — Breach intimation to DPBI and data principals
categories.governance: Sections 8-10 — Data fiduciary and processor obligations
categories.sdf: Section 10 — Significant Data Fiduciary obligations (DPIA, DPO)
categories.children: Section 9 — Processing of children's personal data

controls.title: Pre-collection Notice
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must provide notice to the Data Principal before or at the time of collecting personal data, stating (a) the personal data to be collected and the purpose; (b) how to exercise rights; (c) how to make a complaint to the DPBI.
controls.evidence_expectation: Privacy notice document; UI screenshot at collection point; version history showing notice presented prior to data collection
controls.assessment_procedure: Verify notice precedes or accompanies data collection; check all required elements present; test on web, app, and API channels

controls.title: Notice Language Accessibility
controls.text: The notice must be available in English and in any language specified in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution upon request by the Data Principal.
controls.evidence_expectation: Language versions of privacy notice; process for providing translated notice on request
controls.assessment_procedure: Test language request mechanism; verify at least English and one Eighth Schedule language available

controls.title: Valid Consent Requirements
controls.text: Consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous with a clear affirmative action, limited to data necessary for the specified purpose.
controls.evidence_expectation: Consent UI showing affirmative action (no pre-ticked boxes); consent request limited to stated purpose; consent log samples
controls.assessment_procedure: Review consent UI for pre-ticking, bundled consent, or coercive mechanisms; verify purpose specificity; check consent records are stored with timestamp and purpose

controls.title: Consent Withdrawal
controls.text: A Data Principal must be able to withdraw consent at any time with the same ease as giving it. Withdrawal does not affect lawfulness of processing before withdrawal.
controls.evidence_expectation: Consent withdrawal UI or API; audit log showing withdrawal processed; evidence that withdrawal triggers data cessation or retention review
controls.assessment_procedure: Test withdrawal flow ease; verify downstream processing stops after withdrawal; check audit log; confirm no dark patterns preventing withdrawal

controls.title: Legitimate Use Bases
controls.text: Personal data may be processed without consent for: performance of a contract, compliance with law, medical emergency, employment purposes, or public interest as notified.
controls.evidence_expectation: Legal basis register for each processing activity; documented justification where legitimate use is claimed
controls.assessment_procedure: Verify each processing activity has a documented lawful basis; check legitimate use claims are appropriately narrow and not used to bypass consent

controls.title: Purpose Limitation and Data Minimisation
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary may only process personal data for the purpose for which consent was given or legitimate use applies, and only such data as is necessary for that purpose.
controls.evidence_expectation: Data flow diagrams; Records of Processing Activities (RoPA); evidence that collected data fields are limited to stated purpose
controls.assessment_procedure: Review data collection forms and APIs for excess fields; check RoPA purpose entries match actual processing; verify no secondary use without fresh consent

controls.title: Data Accuracy and Storage Limitation
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must ensure accuracy and completeness of personal data, and must not retain it beyond the period necessary for the purpose or as required by law.
controls.evidence_expectation: Data retention schedule per category; automated deletion or archival policy; evidence of deletion procedures; data quality validation controls
controls.assessment_procedure: Review retention schedules per data type and sector law; test automated deletion; check data quality mechanisms; verify deletion when purpose fulfilled

controls.title: Security Safeguards
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security of personal data against unauthorised collection, processing, use, alteration, disclosure, or destruction.
controls.evidence_expectation: Information security policy covering personal data; encryption at rest and in transit evidence; access control and MFA records; penetration test report (within 12 months); vulnerability management records
controls.assessment_procedure: Review security policy for personal data coverage; verify encryption for PII databases; check access controls; review pentest scope includes personal data systems

controls.title: Breach Notification
controls.text: In case of a personal data breach, the Data Fiduciary must notify the DPBI and each affected Data Principal without delay in the prescribed manner.
controls.evidence_expectation: Incident response plan with breach notification SOP; notification templates for DPBI and data principals; tabletop exercise record (within 12 months)
controls.assessment_procedure: Review IRP for DPBI notification trigger and timeline; verify notification content meets Act requirements; check data principal notification capability at scale; confirm drill or tabletop conducted

controls.title: Data Processor Obligations and DPAs
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must ensure that data processors process personal data only under written instructions and with appropriate security safeguards. Processors must notify the fiduciary of any breach immediately.
controls.evidence_expectation: Data Processing Agreements with all processors; processor security assessment records; DPAs include breach notification clause and purpose limitation
controls.assessment_procedure: Identify all data processors; verify written DPAs exist; check DPAs include purpose limitation, security, breach notification obligations; review processor security assessments

controls.title: Children's Data — Verifiable Parental Consent
controls.text: Before processing personal data of a child (under 18), the Data Fiduciary must obtain verifiable consent from the parent or lawful guardian.
controls.evidence_expectation: Age verification mechanism; parental consent flow; proof that consent is verifiable (not self-certified); child data inventory; technical age gate
controls.assessment_procedure: Test age verification system; review parental consent for verifiability; check age gate cannot be trivially bypassed; verify child data is not used for tracking or profiling

controls.title: Children's Data — Prohibited Processing
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must not process personal data of a child in a manner likely to cause detrimental effect, or track, monitor, target, or profile children.
controls.evidence_expectation: Evidence children's data excluded from behavioural advertising and profiling; product feature assessment against child harm criteria
controls.assessment_procedure: Review advertising and analytics platforms for child data exclusion; verify under-18 data not used for targeting; check product features against detrimental effect criteria

controls.title: Significant Data Fiduciary — DPIA
controls.text: A Significant Data Fiduciary must undertake a Data Protection Impact Assessment for high-risk processing activities and report results to the DPBI as required.
controls.evidence_expectation: SDF determination evidence; DPIA methodology; completed DPIAs for high-risk processing; DPBI reporting records
controls.assessment_procedure: Verify SDF determination is documented; check DPIAs completed for high-risk activities (AI/ML, large-scale health data, biometrics); review DPIA methodology; confirm DPBI reporting compliance

controls.title: Significant Data Fiduciary — Data Protection Officer
controls.text: A Significant Data Fiduciary must appoint a Data Protection Officer who reports to the board, is based in India, and is publicly contactable.
controls.evidence_expectation: DPO appointment letter; DPO reporting line to board documented; India residency evidence; public DPO contact page
controls.assessment_procedure: Verify DPO appointment; check reporting to board not just CISO; confirm India residency; test public contactability via website; check DPO independence

controls.title: Right to Access
controls.text: A Data Principal has the right to obtain a summary of personal data being processed, the processing activities, identities of data processors, and data shared with other fiduciaries.
controls.evidence_expectation: Access request SOP; technical mechanism for providing data summary; SLA; sample fulfilled access request (anonymised)
controls.assessment_procedure: Test access request mechanism end-to-end; verify response contains all required information; check SLA adherence; confirm no unreasonable fee

controls.title: Right to Correction and Erasure
controls.text: A Data Principal has the right to correction, completion, updating, and erasure of personal data. The Data Fiduciary must act within the prescribed period.
controls.evidence_expectation: Correction/erasure SOP; technical erasure mechanism with confirmation; SLA tracking; records of fulfilled requests including cascading deletion to processors
controls.assessment_procedure: Test correction and erasure flows; verify actual deletion from all systems; check cascading deletion to processors; review SLA compliance records

controls.title: Grievance Redressal
controls.text: A Data Fiduciary must establish an effective grievance redressal mechanism and address grievances within the period prescribed by the Board.
controls.evidence_expectation: Grievance officer designation; public-facing portal or email; grievance SLA; sample resolved grievances with response timelines
controls.assessment_procedure: Verify grievance contact publicly accessible; test grievance submission; review SLA against DPBI prescribed period; sample resolved grievances for adequacy

controls.title: Cross-Border Data Transfer
controls.text: Personal data may be transferred outside India only to countries notified by the Central Government. Data Fiduciaries must maintain records of cross-border transfers.
controls.evidence_expectation: Data transfer mapping; list of recipient countries; whitelist compliance evidence or equivalent safeguards; DPAs with foreign processors
controls.assessment_procedure: Map all cross-border personal data flows; verify receiving countries are on approved list; check DPAs with foreign processors include DPDP-equivalent protections
